revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Gorōyama kofun]][[ja:五郎山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Gorōyama Kofun | native_name =五郎山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Goroyama-kofun Tumulus.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Gorōyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Gorōyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.45194444|N|130.54555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Chikushino|qid=Q844140|1=Chikushino, Fukuoka}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|27|07|N|130|32|43|E}}}}}} [[File:Gorōyama Kofun (building).jpg|thumb|right|200px|Detail from the rear wall of the Gorōyama kofun (c. 550-600); building with a painted red door and flared {{ill|Gable|en|Gable|qid=Q1161370|lt=Gable|1=Gable}}s]]
The {{nihongo|'''Gorōyama Kofun'''|五郎山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Decorated kofun]] [[Kofun]], located in the Harada neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Chikushino, Fukuoka|en|Chikushino, Fukuoka|qid=Q844140|lt= Chikushino|1=Chikushino, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1949, with the area under protection adjusted in 1984. <ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139514|title=五郎山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref> It is one of the largest circular tombs in Chikushino, dating from the late Kofun period (6th century).
==Overview== The Sendō Kofun is located on a hill called Mount Gorō, and is an {{nihongo|enpun|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tumulus approximately 35 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. It was discovered by chance in 1947 and in 1948 and a mural was discovered inside the horizontal-pit type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref name="db">{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/index.php?g=d_frame.php&index=&SEQNO=&PCD=40&KNO=22&modori=mjl |title=五郎山古墳 |trans-title=Gorōyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=1 April 2012}}</ref><ref name="Museum">{{cite web |url=http://www.city.chikushino.fukuoka.jp/furusato/kofunkan.htm |title=五郎山古墳館 |trans-title=Museum of the Gorōyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher={{ill|Chikushino, Fukuoka|en|Chikushino, Fukuoka|qid=Q844140|lt=Chikushino City|1=Chikushino, Fukuoka}} |accessdate=1 April 2012}}</ref> The burial chamber with a total length of 11.2 meters, and is a multi-chamber structure unique to northern Kyushu, consisting of a passage, anterior chamber, and posterior chamber. The murals are painted along the passageway from the anterior chamber to the posterior chamber, and throughout the posterior chamber. The designs are painted in red, black, and green and depict people, ships, cavalry, houses, horses, snowflakes, animals, and concentric circles, but many have faded and is now difficult to decipher.<ref name="db"/><ref>{{cite book |title=Early Japanese Art: the great tombs and treasures |author=Ki11er, Edward J |publisher=[[Thames and Hudson]] |year=1964 |pages=123, 310–2}}</ref> Due to grave robbing, very few grave goods remained, but gold rings, pipe beads, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, swords, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} have been collected.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> <ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/226348/ |title=五郎山古墳 |trans-title=Gorōyama kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=Iseki Walker |accessdate=1 April 2012}}</ref> The tomb is dated to c. 550-600.<ref name="db"/>
The site is approximately a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Harada Station|en|Harada Station|qid=Q5370054|lt=Harada Station|1=Harada Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|qid=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}. There is a museum on site<ref name="Shiseki"/>The {{nihongo|Gorōyama Kofun Museum|五郎山古墳館|Gorōyama kofunkan}} exhibits replicas of the paintings and has an observation room with a view into the mound.<ref name="Museum"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Goroyama Kofun}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=11 Cultural Properties in Fukuoka]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.chikushino.fukuoka.jp/soshiki/48/3484.html Chikushino City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12585 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.kyuhaku.jp/dazaifu/d-map/kaisetu27.html Kyushu National Museum]{{in lang|ja}}
Gorōyama kofun
